ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Jan-26-2009 Continued To Mar-09-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Dean Coggins for and with Mary Elizabeth Thomas (Objector). Robert Roddick for and with Petitioners.

Mr. Roddick addressed the issue regarding the retirement benefits. The parties agreed to attend mediation. The Court referred this matter to Pro Bono Mediation (signing an order for the same in open court). Mediation must be completed no later than February 27, 2009. The parties were instructed to inform the Probate Examiner of the results of the mediation by March 3, 2009. The matter was continued to March 9, 2009 for status on the mediation. The Court advised all parties that it would be best to mediate on all outstanding issues as there are limited funds in the estate.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/ Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy J. Shannon.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Dec-08-2008 Continued To Jan-26-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Roddick for Marie Brown. Dena Coggins for and with Mary Elizabeth Thomas.

The Court was informed that the parties did not reach settlement on any of the issues. Mr. Roddick stated that he attempted to combine the estate, trust, and bank accounts and that his client did not realize that the property was removed from the trust by the decedent. Ms. Coggins requested the Court authorize the proceeds from the sale of the trust property be placed into an interest bearing account, which the Court granted (Ms. Coggins is to prepare the order). The matter was continued to January 26, 2009. Mr. Roddick was instructed to provide proof of title on the bank accounts, closing statements on the $100,000.00 loan, and submit a proposed order. Ms. Brown is to amend the accounting which is to include the funds in the Bank of America accounts. The parties are not willing to participate in mediation but are willing to entertain settlement. The Court waived Ms. Thomas' appearance at the next hearing.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: J. Alameda./R. Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Filing Of Marie Brown'S Accounting Petition
Off Calendar Sep-02-2008 Continued To Sep-23-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Roddick for Marie Johnson Brown.

Mr. Roddick requested a continuance stating that he was missing 3 bank statements from Bank of America and 1 from Bank Of the West. The matter was continued to September 23, 2008 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of Marie Brown's accounting.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Leticia Perez CSR#7782. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy S. Neu.

Petition For Breaches Of Trust, For Damages Or Surcharge, For Removal Of Trustee, For Appointment Of Temporary Trustee, And For Order Confirming Trust Asset/Quiet Title
Off Calendar Aug-06-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Roddick for Marie Brown and Nehamiah Brown. Paul Marchegiani for Mary Thomas.

Mr. Roddick requested a continuance stating that he had just been retained as Counsel. Mr. Marchegiani objected to the continuance stating that a sale of the property was pending and that the sale and the property were in jeopardy of being lost. The Court directed the parties to meet ouside the Courtroom and try to resolve the matter. Upon returning, the parties stated that not all issues were resolved and requested direction from the Court. After hearing oral argument, the Court ordered the following: 1) The Court will set aside the deed transferring the property to Marie Brown and Nehamiah Brown and make the property part of the trust. 2) Marie Brown will resign as Trustee and Mary Thomas will be appointed as Successor Trustee. 3) The Court ordered an accounting by Marie Brown which will cover the entire time of her tenure. 4) The distribution issue will not be addressed until all figures have been reviewed. 5) The proceeds from the sale of the property will be held in escrow until further order of the court. 6) Marie Brown and Nehamiah Brown are to vacate the premises by August 15, 2008. Mr. Marchegiani will prepare the order. Status on Ms. Brown's accounting is set for September 2, 2008.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Hill.

Petition For Breaches Of Trust, For Damages Or Surcharge, For Removal Of Trustee, For Appointment Of Temporary Trustee, And For Order Confirming Trust Asset/Quiet Title
